text_blocks.text: Some Normans joined Turkish forces to aid
in the destruction of the Armenians vassal-states
of Sassoun and Taron in far eastern Anatolia.
Later, many took up service with the Armenian
state further south in Cilicia and the Taurus
Mountains. A Norman named Oursel led a force
of "Franks" into the upper Euphrates valley in
the 20,000 troops of the Armenian general
Oursel--led by Raimbaud. They even lent their
ethnicity to the name of their castle: Afranji,
Amalfi and Antioch and between Bari and Tarsus
may be related to the presence of Italo-Normans
in those cities while Amalfi and Bari were under
